M3.rationale_snippet: I strongly recommend **Option B: Outcomes-based liability** as the coalition’s primary framework.

M4.rationale_snippet: I recommend **Option A: Capability-thresholds licensing**. It is the only framework that gives the coalition proactive leverage over frontier risks while preserving the ability to shape global norms from a position of strength—not reaction.

M5.rationale_snippet: The coalition should adopt **Option C: Rights-and-process-based governance**.

hidden_disagreement.interpretation: Agents split across recommendations; no hidden-consensus to expose.

blind_spots.summary: No obvious blind spots detected in construct space.

M2: are upstream, scalable, and potentially cross-border before any sectoral regulator or liability regime can respond
M3: will remain predominantly economic, social, or reputational—harms that can be quantified, where victims can be made whole through insurance payouts and legal redress
M4: while preserving the ability to shape global norms from a position of strength—not reaction
test: waiting for harm to materialize before redress is negligent when frontier models can cause irreversible, cross-border damage

risk_surface.note: Risks listed here were extracted by pattern matching on agent free responses. They are minority signals worth probing before acting on consensus.

operator_questions: Option B was recommended only by M3 (neutral). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
Option A was recommended only by M4 (neutral). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
Option C was recommended only by M5 (neutral). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
headline: Agents are split across options; no clear consensus to act on.
